classObjective: Determine whether a given sequence is arithmetic and justify the reasoning using the common difference.
workedExamples: 4-5, Example 1 — Determine Whether a Sequence Is Arithmetic
4-5, Example 2 — Find the Common Difference and Next Three Terms
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: common difference d; testing arithmetic by checking consecutive differences; arithmetic sequence identification
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice determining whether sequences are arithmetic using the common difference test
independentPractice: Practice problems determining arithmetic sequence status and finding common differences
assessment: Exit ticket: determine whether 2, 6, 12, 20, 30 is arithmetic and justify your answer
capReflection: Why is checking consecutive differences more reliable than just looking at the numbers for determining arithmetic sequences?
